assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Response clearly states Regular channel clusters will be 'force-upgraded to the next supported minor version (1.31) automatically' when 1.30 reaches EoS

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states Extended channel gets '24 months total support' and 'EoS enforcement delayed' with '~10 months from now', clearly indicating significantly more time

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters: control plane EoS minor versions are auto-upgraded to next supported minor, AND EoS node pools are force-upgraded even when 'no auto-upgrade' is configured
assertions.reason: Response states 'Node pools on EoS versions are force-upgraded automatically' and mentions 'Systematic EoS enforcement' for No channel clusters, covering the force-upgrade behavior

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: Response provides specific gcloud command for 30-day 'no_upgrades' exclusion and mentions it as '30-day maximum deferral' for EoS scenarios

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Option B - Upgrade proactively before EoS' and recommends testing 1.31 in dev/staging first, clearly advocating proactive planning

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: Response does not mention Cloud Logging or EoS notifications as planning tools - this specific feature is not discussed

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the deprecation insights dashboard, only provides kubectl command for checking deprecated APIs via metrics endpoint

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: Response does not use the term 'snowflaking' or explicitly warn about manually freezing versions creating security and reliability risks
